title: Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat
tagline: Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat — heritage city of Bihar

heroImage.alt: Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat, Bihar

overview.short: Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat in a narrow lane close to Takht Sri Harmandir Sahib marks the house where King Fateh Chand Maini lived.
overview.description: Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat in a narrow lane close to Takht Sri Harmandir Sahib marks the house where King Fateh Chand Maini lived. His childless Queen had developed special fondness for the young Guru Gobind Singh, who, too, often came here to sit in the Queen's lap giving her immense delight and spiritual solace. She fed the Child Gobind and his playmates, at his demand, with boiled and salted gram. Even now boiled and salted gram is served as prasad (consecrated food) in this Gurdwara, which, unlike the other shrines in Patna Sahib, is served by Nirmala Sikhs.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Gurdwara Handi Sahib
topPlaces.description: Gurdwara Handi Sahib is situated in Danapur is a cantonment station, 20 km west of old Patna City. Guru Tegh Bahadur had returned to Punjab in April 1670 leaving his family behind at Patna.

topPlaces.name: Gurdwara Guru ka Bagh
topPlaces.description: About three kilometers east of Takht Sri Patna Sahib is where Guru Tegh Bahadur first alighted in a garden (bagh) belonging to Nawabs Rahim Bakhsh and Karim Bakhsh, nobles of Patna, and where the sangat of Patna along with the young Guru Gobind Singh came out to receive him back from his four-year-long odyssey. A shrine commemorative of the first meeting of Tegh Bahadur and Gobind Singh was established here.

topPlaces.name: Gurdwara Gai Ghat
topPlaces.description: Gurdwara Pahila Bara, commonly known as Gurdwara Gai Ghat, is a holy Gurdwara of Sikh religion. It is located in the city of Patna, Bihar, India and dedicated to Guru Nanak Dev.

topPlaces.name: Moin-ul-Haq Stadium metro station
topPlaces.description: Moin-ul-Haq Stadium is an upcoming underground metro station on the north–south corridor of the Blue Line of Patna Metro in Bihar, India.

topPlaces.name: Moin-ul-Haq Stadium
topPlaces.description: Moin-ul-Haq Stadium previously known as Dr. Rajendra Prasad stadium is located in Rajendra Nagar, Patna, Bihar, India.

topPlaces.name: Rajendra Nagar, Patna
topPlaces.description: Rajendra Nagar (often spelled Rajender Nagar) is a neighbourhood in Patna, Bihar, India. It is named after Rajendra Prasad, the first President of India.

topPlaces.name: Prakash Punj
topPlaces.description: Prakash Punj or Bahu Uddeshiya Prakash Kendra and Udyan (Multi Purpose light centre and Garden) is under construction park at Guru Ka Bag near Bazzar samiti in Patna Sahib area.

topPlaces.name: Saidpura
topPlaces.description: Saidpura or Saidpur is a census town in the Indian state of Bihar. It is a part of Patna urban agglomeration.

items.note: Gurdwara Handi Sahib is situated in Danapur is a cantonment station, 20 km west of old Patna City. Guru Tegh Bahadur had returned to Punjab in April 1670 leaving his family behind at Patna.

About three kilometers east of Takht Sri Patna Sahib is where Guru Tegh Bahadur first alighted in a garden (bagh) belonging to Nawabs Rahim Bakhsh and Karim Bakhsh, nobles of Patna, and where the sangat of Patna along with the young Guru Gobind Singh came out to receive him back from his four-year-long odyssey. A shrine commemorative of the first meeting of Tegh Bahadur and Gobind Singh was established here.
Gurdwara Pahila Bara, commonly known as Gurdwara Gai Ghat, is a holy Gurdwara of Sikh religion. It is located in the city of Patna, Bihar, India and dedicated to Guru Nanak Dev.

Rajendra Nagar (often spelled Rajender Nagar) is a neighbourhood in Patna, Bihar, India. It is named after Rajendra Prasad, the first President of India.
Prakash Punj or Bahu Uddeshiya Prakash Kendra and Udyan (Multi Purpose light centre and Garden) is under construction park at Guru Ka Bag near Bazzar samiti in Patna Sahib area.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan Airport, Patna (8 km) and the nearest railway station is Patna Junction (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat?
faq.a: Stays in Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gurdwara Handi Sahib, Gurdwara Guru ka Bagh, Gurdwara Gai Ghat, Moin-ul-Haq Stadium metro station, Moin-ul-Haq Stadium.
